run_metadata.notes: Threat model (dashboard + worker): (1) Authenticated users access project data via Next API and Supabase RLS; mis-merged engine/orchestration data is integrity/consistency, not direct auth bypass. (2) Operator CLIs and default DB URLs risk accidental connections to unintended clusters. (3) Broad table GRANTs with RLS remain contingent on policy correctness (tracked in f-58c68aa7). (4) No new auth bypass identified in this pass.

agent.name: security-and-privacy-auditor
agent.role: Identify practical security and privacy risks for worker CLI and dashboard data paths in the 20260410-182313 scope.

findings.title: Worker admin CLI embeds default local DATABASE_URL when env unset
findings.description: Attack / exposure scenario: an operator runs `npx tsx src/scripts/admin.ts` without exporting DATABASE_URL. The script silently connects to the hard-coded postgresql://penny:penny-dev-password@localhost:5432/penny. If a machine has a different service on that port, or the operator assumed the CLI was a no-op dry run, commands could mutate the wrong database or leak that dev password pattern into shared runbooks. Prefer failing fast unless DATABASE_URL is set, or read from a documented local-only dotenv.

proof_hooks.summary: Default connection string when process.env.DATABASE_URL falsy
Unset DATABASE_URL, run admin.ts health from worker package; observe connection to localhost default.

suggested_fix.approach: Require explicit DATABASE_URL for non-local subcommands or gate defaults behind NODE_ENV=development.
steps: Remove embedded password default for production paths

next_actions.action: Fail fast or prompt when DATABASE_URL missing; move dev defaults to .env.example only.
Reconcile f-58c68aa7 (repair_jobs GRANT) with narrowed-GRANT decision and integration tests.
next_actions.rationale: Accepted finding; blast radius if RLS regresses.

next_actions.action: After fixing f-af487c6c, confirm no sensitive fields leak via mismatched orchestration vs engine payloads.
next_actions.rationale: Consistency finding may hide future authz assumptions.
